$schema_notes: Each project defines its ceiling: the maximum capability it needs to support. Tool selection checks whether a tool's capability envelope contains the ceiling. Re-evaluate only when a ceiling changes, not when a new tool appears.

KD-GAT.description: CAN bus intrusion detection — ML research pipeline on HPC

experiment_tracking.description: Track experiments across 5 researchers, compare runs, reproduce from metadata
trigger_to_reevaluate: Databricks restricts OSS MLflow tracking server features
Need real-time collaborative experiment analysis (MLflow UI is read-only)
Team exceeds 10 concurrent writers on SQLite/PostgreSQL backend

orchestration.description: Multi-stage pipeline with HPO, multi-seed, multi-dataset fan-out on SLURM
trigger_to_reevaluate: Ray drops SLURM support or breaks compatibility
Need orchestration across multiple HPC clusters
Ray head-node overhead exceeds 10% of job allocation memory

hyperparameter_tuning.description: Distributed HPO with early stopping, multi-fidelity, parallel trials
trigger_to_reevaluate: Ray Tune dropped or incompatible with Optuna
Need HPO across heterogeneous hardware (GPU types)

training_framework.description: PyTorch training with callbacks, mixed precision, multi-GPU, checkpointing
trigger_to_reevaluate: Lightning AI deprioritizes OSS Trainer for cloud platform
Lightning breaks PyG DataLoader compatibility
Need custom training loops that fight the Trainer abstraction

config_management.description: Typed, frozen, composable config with YAML files and CLI overrides
trigger_to_reevaluate: Need distributed config serving (multiple services reading config)
Config schema exceeds what Pydantic can validate (conditional cross-field)

data_versioning.description: Track raw datasets, preprocessed caches, and model artifacts
trigger_to_reevaluate: Need fine-grained data lineage (which rows trained which model)
Dataset size exceeds NFS quota and needs external storage
data_versioning.notes: DVC is passive dead weight. Evaluate whether Git LFS + HF Hub alone covers the ceiling.

model_serving.description: REST API for inference, health checks, batch prediction
trigger_to_reevaluate: Need A/B testing or canary deployments
Need GPU-optimized batching (dynamic batching, continuous batching)
Deploying to edge devices (not just REST)

dashboard.description: Ops dashboard for experiment comparison, training curves, model analysis
trigger_to_reevaluate: Need real-time updates (websocket, not poll)
Need multi-page app with authentication
Dashboard becomes public-facing (not just lab internal)
dashboard.notes: 5 switches in this category. Accept that dashboard is non-core and optimize for lowest maintenance, not highest capability.

ci_cd.description: Lint, test subset on push, full test suite on SLURM
trigger_to_reevaluate: Need CI on private HPC (no GitHub-hosted runners)
GitHub Actions minutes become a cost issue

database.description: Metrics storage, sweep state, MLflow backend
trigger_to_reevaluate: SQLite NFS corruption under concurrent writes
PostgreSQL Apptainer job maintenance burden exceeds value
Need time-series specific queries (not just relational)

package_management.description: Fast, reproducible Python environment with pinned ML dependencies
trigger_to_reevaluate: Need non-Python dependencies managed (C libraries, CUDA toolkit)
uv drops PyTorch/PyG wheel compatibility

containerization.description: Reproducible environments, PostgreSQL hosting, dashboard deployment
trigger_to_reevaluate: HPC switches to Podman/other runtime
Need multi-container orchestration on HPC (unlikely)

Map-Visualizations.description: Ohio political district maps + campaign finance — interactive web dashboards

data_pipeline.description: FEC/census data → DuckDB → static site with interactive charts
trigger_to_reevaluate: Dataset exceeds browser memory (~500MB)
Need real-time data updates (not batch)
Need server-side query execution

visualization.description: Interactive maps, network graphs, statistical charts for publication + web
trigger_to_reevaluate: Need 3D visualization (deck.gl territory)
Need collaborative editing (Observable notebooks)
D3 maintenance slows (unlikely, very stable)
Need server-side rendering or API routes
Need authentication for private dashboards

data_quality.description: Validate FEC/census data integrity on ingest
trigger_to_reevaluate: Need cross-source validation (FEC vs census join integrity)
Data sources exceed 5 (currently 2-3)

when_to_evaluate: A trigger condition fires (listed per capability)
A ceiling changes (e.g., team grows, new deployment target)
Annual review (low-effort: check community_momentum for all current tools)
when_NOT_to_evaluate: A new tool is trending on social media
A current tool has a minor annoyance
Someone at a conference recommends something
how_to_evaluate: 1. Confirm the trigger is real (not frustration-driven)
2. Check project_ceilings.json — has the ceiling actually changed?
3. Query mlops_tools_catalog.json for tools matching the capability requirements
4. Filter candidates: capability_ceiling >= project ceiling level, migration_likelihood = low
5. Prefer tools where composite_tool = false (standalone) unless composing with an already-adopted tool
6. Check migration_cost from current tool — must be justified by ceiling coverage gap, not feature count
7. Check lock_in_risk — acceptable if migration_likelihood is low (you won't need to leave)
8. Verify community_momentum != declining — a dying tool forces migration regardless of fit

key_dimensions_for_selection.capability_ceiling: Does the tool cover where the project is going? extensive > high > medium > low
key_dimensions_for_selection.migration_likelihood: How likely are we to outgrow this tool? Low = keep, high = avoid
key_dimensions_for_selection.lock_in_risk: Cost to leave IF we must. Acceptable when migration_likelihood is low
key_dimensions_for_selection.composite_tool: Is this a standalone solution or part of a stack? Standalone = simpler to reason about
key_dimensions_for_selection.community_momentum: Declining = forced migration eventually. Growing = safe bet

evaluation_protocol.migration_budget: Max 2 tool switches per quarter across all projects. If you're hitting this limit, the ceilings are wrong, not the tools.
evaluation_protocol.history: See ~/tool-migration-history.md — 30 switches in 14 months. The framework exists to end this pattern.
